title: EFT: Trauma Retelling
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides extracted from the EFT interventions practice deck.

sources.label: Vrana and Greenberg, Overview of Emotion-Focused Therapy

slides.title: Marker: a broken or avoided story
slides.body: Use when the event is present in fragments and the client can stay dual-aware.
bullets: Confirm consent, pacing, and exit signals before entering.
Keep one foot in the room and one foot in the memory.
Stop when new meaning is forming, not when the story is exhausted.

slides.title: Titrate the narrative
slides.body: Move in and out of the memory with active therapist presence. The client should never be alone inside the event.
steps: Set the frame: what scene, why now, and how to stop.
Begin before the worst moment; locate body, image, and orientation.
Retell in short segments; pause for emotion and meaning after each turn.
Heighten adaptive anger, grief, or protective impulse when it appears.
Close with what the client knows now, needs now, and can reclaim.

slides.title: Signs the work is productive
slides.body: Retelling is on track when the narrative becomes more alive and more owned, not merely more detailed.
items: The client can name what happened and what it meant then.
The body shows waves of activation and settling.
Primary emotion replaces numbness, collapse, or global shame.
A new stance appears: protection, protest, grief, dignity, or self-contact.
